item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced, and thus is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know their hive mind is not only there greatest strength but greatest weakness. If they ever get too out of hand and the foundation decides to destroy them all all they would need to do is show one of them a slow acting(few minutes) memetic kill agent, in which case it will spread to every last one of them

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noticed a plot hole.
Our friend -1 is not exempted from Protocol-G7. So contrary to the last memo The Foundation is not accumulating copies, sympathetic or otherwise.

From the mention of "sedated instances" in the memo, it seems like there was a separate procedure that got cut at some point. Probably a variant of G7 tailored for -1 that involves keeping that branch of clones in a drugged coma instead of euthanasia.

Could be fixed by slipping in a line or two about such a variation. But that's not a minor edit, especially for a +100 article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the exact same thing! Well, that and Gary from Fallout 3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how this is basically Horatio from "Endless Space". Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If they're willing to destroy the anomaly for safety reasons, they could track down his mom and administer a unicorn horn tablet.
If not, they could use the image that changes your history into a Foundation employee.
Both of these can affect his timeline before all of the splits; his collective mind doesn't need to be capable of transmitting memetic effects. The image might still decommission him if it decides it needs to remove his anomalous nature to make him a good candidate for the job, especially if the source of his anomalousness was a random spontaneous event, because "It is suspected that this anomaly follows a 'path of least resistance' and alters the minimum necessary number of events," so causing a spontaneous event to not happen would be a likely change.
If they really want to, I'm sure there are SCPs that can extract him from a time when he was unsplit, if that doesn't cause paradoxes.
There might be telepathic things that can order all the copies through the collective mind network to wait somewhere for collection (a very unsettling sight for civilians though), which would probably be the cheapest way to collect them intact, if that's an option.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs a tale! Upvoted for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This guy is clever. How can the Foundation be sure that the "good" one didn't turn himself in in order to gain info about any groups that might oppose him? He gives away positions of some instances (easily replaced), and in turn gains their trust. Which means, the Foundation may have been tricked into creating an army of these guys internally, thinking they'll help when no longer sedated, while instead they'll attack the Foundation from the inside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So why not use another visual cognitohazard that doesn't have the risk of a breach? Just expose one to a hazard like the one seen on the 001 page. Since they all share memories, they all die. In addition, nobody else is at a direct risk from the kill agent.

Of course, the Foundation isn't simply going to destroy an anomaly, but still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seeing as they share consciousness, that is just crazy enough to work. Of course, there is the small detail of not knowing just where SCP-1157 instances are, and what news cameras would be recording the resulting carnage. So there is the issue of containment breach of security as 096 rampages across the contry/world, possably losing the bag over its head on the prowess …

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If they were really connected, 1157 could be simply neutralized by exposing to photo of 096 while 096's face is safely covered during the big fat killing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope someone can further develop this man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These guys could form an army and take over something very easily. Or form a nation of "me's". Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Terrorist cell.

+ show block
– hide block

skeleton playing a sad trombone

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realize I've commented before, but I just re-read this and I have to say - this is probably one of my favorite SCPs. It works SO well, and manages to be a genuinely-terrifying humanoid SCP without falling into "humanoid what is dangerous and can kill you" territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't up vote. Too many humanoid copy things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … this one is probably uncontainable. And yet, upvoted. : P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More dangerous than the cakes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of the skips I've seen, I never thought one with such a boring concept could actual scare me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn…. that was just scary at the end. I congratulate you sir!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This actually works really well, in my opinion. As they share mindsets, any Foundation info leaked to them could instantly be transferred to an outside instance, and then… erhm.
The picture also looks suspiciously like an old teacher of mine O__O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfectly said. Everything about this concept is boring, right down to using a standard-issue generic white dude. And yet it's pretty damn entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this a lot, don't care that it's been done before! It does it just as well. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for very interesting execution of a potentially boring idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're sedating new duplicates of the good ones, that are separated from the others they have in containment. They're euthanizing new duplicates of the bad ones that they've captured.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. One would think that Able would love this, except that he's busy being locked in his box, a comfortable distance from any other SCP besides maybe Leviathan. An enemy that never dies, constantly fighting him.

Seriously, I think he'd be thrilled. Keep his daily dose of opponents high, blood knight's gotta stay healthy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: According to the end note they want more sedated instances, but in the middle it says they're euthanising new formed duplicates. Might want to clear that issue up IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gave SCP-1157 individual personalities but minor shared consciousness. Modified containment procedures. Edited/added logs and added interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, but then you're running up against the four guys who share memories.

I think this is a great idea, at least superficially different from 493, but the writing could use some work. It feels like you're really rushing to get everything down and get to the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. A narcissistic bastard who just duplicates. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, I'm working on a rewrite that will give SCP-1157 a limited shared awareness that will hopefully differentiate it more from SCP-493. It should be done by the end of the day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is exactly what I was thinking. Other than the fact that they duplicate much more rapidly, this is essentially identical to 493.

Although this is very well written and thought out, I'm sorry to say that on reflection I'm going to have to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We already have some duplicate people with a shared memory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm wondering whether modifying them to share a consciousness would add or detract to the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upgraded to Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Not sure how I feel about this. The execution is pretty good, and the image of hordes of clones fighting Foundation task forces is amusing, but…we kind of already have this. 1867 was different enough from 1845 that I can upvote both, but personally I'm not sure about this one…

Also, I do feel it should be Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was considering Keter. Maybe I'll add a note requesting an upgrade to Keter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but I think it may be Keter for the same reason SCP-871 is.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! It's actually an idea for a novel I've been kicking around for the past year or so that I suddenly realized would be something the Foundation would want to contain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a very good example of how you take a simple idea (a dude that doubles himself every four weeks) and make it into a damn good SCP. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it be worth following that thread a bit more? Maybe something found by MTF Gamma-7?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was worth having more than just a mugshot. I'll take it out as it doesn't really add anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does it need a picture of the containment area? It's like taking a picture of a prison block and saying "prisoner 42 is kept here, somewhere, I think it's that one on the far left".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The carving on the walls was what did it for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just did. I was struggling to get the images and tags updated. They kept failing for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You haven't announced it yet, have you? Tsk tsk

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New SCP. Open for crits and comments. I'm having trouble uploading the files directly so I'm hosting them remotely for now. Will try again later.
